questions.q: What was the main objective of the British rule in India?
opts: (C) To exploit India's resources for economic growth
(D) To promote Indian culture and language
questions.explanation: The British exploited India's resources for economic growth, which led to India's economic backwardness.

questions.q: Who was the leader of the Indian National Congress in 1885?
questions.explanation: A.O. Hume was the founder of the Indian National Congress, and he played a key role in its early years.

questions.q: What was the significance of the Indian National Congress's 1906 split?
opts: (A) It led to the formation of the Muslim League
(B) It marked the beginning of non-cooperation movement
(C) It resulted in the appointment of the Hunter Commission
(D) It led to the emergence of the Swadeshi movement
questions.explanation: The Indian National Congress's 1906 split led to the formation of the All-India Muslim League, which would later become a major rival to the Congress.

questions.q: Who led the Non-Cooperation Movement in 1920?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi led the Non-Cooperation Movement in 1920, which aimed to boycott British goods and institutions.

questions.q: What was the main purpose of the Simon Commission in 1927?
opts: (A) To inquire into the working of the Government of India Act, 1919
(B) To investigate the causes of the Non-Cooperation Movement
(C) To recommend constitutional reforms in India
(D) To conduct a census of India
questions.explanation: The Simon Commission was sent to India in 1927 to investigate and recommend constitutional reforms in India.

questions.q: Who was the leader of the Congress in 1942?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi was the leader of the Indian National Congress in 1942, when he launched the Quit India Movement.

questions.q: What was the significance of the Cabinet Mission in 1946?
opts: (A) It proposed the partition of India
(B) It recommended the establishment of a federation
(C) It led to the formation of the Interim Government
(D) It resulted in the adoption of the Indian Constitution
questions.explanation: The Cabinet Mission proposed the establishment of a federation in India, which would have given greater autonomy to the provinces.

questions.q: Who played a key role in the formation of the Muslim League in 1906?
questions.explanation: Muhammad Ali Jinnah played a key role in the formation of the All-India Muslim League in 1906.

questions.q: What was the main objective of the Khilafat Movement in 1919?
questions.explanation: The Khilafat Movement was launched in 1919 to protect the Ottoman Caliphate, which was under threat from the Allied Powers.

questions.q: Who was the leader of the Indian National Army during World War II?
questions.explanation: Subhas Chandra Bose was the leader of the Indian National Army, also known as the Azad Hind Fauj, during World War II.

questions.q: What was the significance of the Indian Independence Act in 1947?
opts: (A) It granted dominion status to India
questions.explanation: The Indian Independence Act in 1947 partitioned India and Pakistan, leading to the creation of two separate nations.

questions.q: Who was the founder of the Indian National Congress?
questions.explanation: A.O. Hume was the founder of the Indian National Congress, and he played a key role in its early years.

questions.q: What was the main objective of the Non-Cooperation Movement in 1920?
opts: (C) To boycott British goods and institutions
(D) To reform the Indian National Congress
questions.explanation: The Non-Cooperation Movement in 1920 aimed to boycott British goods and institutions, as a means of securing Indian independence.

questions.q: Who was the leader of the Congress in 1930?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi was the leader of the Indian National Congress in 1930, when he launched the Salt March.

questions.q: What was the significance of the Government of India Act in 1935?
opts: (A) It granted full independence to India
(B) It established a federation in India
(C) It led to the formation of the Interim Government
(D) It resulted in the adoption of the Indian Constitution
questions.explanation: The Government of India Act in 1935 proposed the establishment of a federation in India, which would have given greater autonomy to the provinces.

questions.q: Who played a key role in the formation of the Interim Government in 1946?
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru played a key role in the formation of the Interim Government in 1946, which was headed by Lord Louis Mountbatten.

questions.q: What was the main objective of the Quit India Movement in 1942?
opts: (C) To boycott British goods and institutions
(D) To reform the Indian National Congress
questions.explanation: The Quit India Movement in 1942 aimed to secure Indian independence, and was launched by Mahatma Gandhi.

questions.q: Who was the leader of the Indian National Congress in 1947?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi was the leader of the Indian National Congress in 1947, when India gained independence.

questions.q: What was the significance of the Radcliffe Line in 1947?
opts: (A) It marked the boundary between India and Pakistan
(B) It designated the capital of India
questions.explanation: The Radcliffe Line was a boundary that was drawn by Sir Cyril Radcliffe in 1947, dividing India and Pakistan.

questions.q: Who was the last Viceroy of India?
questions.explanation: Lord Louis Mountbatten was the last Viceroy of India, serving from 1947 to 1948.
